ORDER DENYING DEFENDANT MISSION STUCCO COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 503, DEFENDANT MISSION STUCCO COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED AS MODIFIED: DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILED TO SUSTAIN ITS INITIAL BURDEN UNDER EITHER PRONG OF AGUILAR V. ATLANTIC RICHFIELD CO. (2001) 25 CAL.4TH 826. DEFENDANT FAILED TO SUSTAIN ITS BURDEN OF DEMONSTRATING THAT PLAINTIFFS DO NOT POSSESS AND CANNOT REASONABLY OBTAIN EVIDENCE THAT DECEDENT CLAUDE JOYNER WAS EXPOSED TO ASBESTOS-CONTAINING PRODUCTS OR MATERIALS ATTRIBUTABLE TO DEFENDANT. ERSIE JOYNER'S DEPOSITION TESTIMONY AND PLAINTIFFS' RESPONSES TO DEFENDANT'S SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES PRECLUDE DEFENDANT FROM SUCCESSFULLY INVOKING THE FACTUALLY DEVOID PRONG. DEFENDANT ALSO FAILED TO SUSTAIN ITS BURDEN OF PRESENTING EVIDENCE THAT WOULD REQUIRE A REASONABLE TRIER OF FACT NOT TO FIND ANY UNDERLYING MATERIAL FACT MORE LIKELY THAN NOT. THE DECLARATION OF GILBERT HERNANDEZ FAILS TO CONCLUSIVELY NEGATE AN ELEMENT OF PLAINTIFF'S CAUSES OF ACTION. AT MOST, WHEN READ IN CONJUNCTION WITH ERSIE JOYNER'S DEPOSITION TESTIMONY, IT MERELY CREATES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT. HOWEVER EVEN HAD DEFENDANT SUSTAINED ITS INITIAL BURDEN, THE DEPOSITION TESTIMONY OF ERSIE JOYNER, GILBERT HERNANDEZ AND JAMES WELL, THE DECLARATION OF ARTHUR WOODMAN AND DEFENDANT'S RESPONSES TO GENERAL ORDER NO. 129 INTERROGATORIES WOULD CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER DECEDENT WAS EXPOSED TO ASBESTOS-CONTAINING PRODUCTS OR MATERIALS ATTRIBUTABLE TO DEFENDANT. PLAINTIFFS' OBJECTIONS TO MR. HERNANDEZ'S DECLARATION ARE OVERRULED. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO THE AUGUST 2014 ERSIE JOYNER TESTIMONY ARE SUSTAINED AND ITS OBJECTIONS TO THE SEPTEMBER 2015 ERSIE JOYNER TESTIMONY (55:13-18) ARE OVERRULED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: GARRETT L. WONG, REPORTER: MICHELE LUCAS OF AIKEN WELCH, CSR #4017 (TEL: 510-451-1580)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant Mission Stucco Company - Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Feb-11-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Todd A. Duplanty of SELMAN BREITMAN LLP (415-979-0400), appearing for defendant / moving party Mission Stucco Company
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Ruby Joyner as wrongful death heir, and as successor-in-interest to Claude Joyner, deceased, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT MISSION STUCCO COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted as modified: Defendant Mission Stucco Company's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that decedent Claude Joyner was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Ersie Joyner's deposition testimony and plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong. Defendant also failed to sustain its burden of presenting evidence that would require a reasonable trier of fact not to find any underlying material fact more likely than not. The declaration of Gilbert Hernandez fails to conclusively negate an element of plaintiff's causes of action. At most, when read in conjunction with Ersie Joyner's deposition testimony, it merely creates a triable issue of material fact. However even had defendant sustained its initial burden, the deposition testimony of Ersie Joyner, Gilbert Hernandez and James Well, the declaration of Arthur Woodman and defendant's responses to General Order no. 129 interrogatories would create a triable issue whether decedent was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiffs' objections to Mr. Hernandez's declaration are overruled. Defendant's objections to the August 2014 Ersie Joyner testimony are sustained and its objections to the September 2015 Ersie Joyner testimony (55:13-18) are overruled. Order signed in open court. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Reporter: Michele Lucas of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #4017 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/GLW)
